classified_questions.question: Can you describe the first step in solving this problem using the given logarithm property $\log x+\log y=\log xy$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What does it mean when we rewrite $\log_{10} 40+\log_{10} 25$ as $\log_{10}(40\cdot 25)$? How does this help us simplify the expression?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we rewrite $40 \cdot 25$ as a power of 10? What does that tell us about the value of $\log_{10}(40\cdot 25)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What value of $x$ satisfies the equation $10^x = 1000$? How does this value relate to the expression $\log_{10} 40+\log_{10} 25$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the expression $\log_{10} 40+\log_{10} 25$ simplifies to $\log_{10}(40 \cdot 25) = \log 1000$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What does $\log 1000$ equal? How does this final value relate to the original expression $\log_{10} 40+\log_{10} 25$?
